name: CampusOS
tagline: Unified student services and activity platform for campus life
description: A modular, multi-tenant Campus Operating System for managing the complete lifecycle of campus activities, including clubs, events, tasks, operations, and growth.

repositories.name: CampusOS
repositories.description: Unified monorepo containing both frontend, backend, apps, and documentation.

problemStatement: Campus activity management is fragmented and chaotic, relying on scattered WhatsApp messages, inconsistent Google Sheets, and email chains. This results in coordination chaos, data duplication, vendor fragmentation, lack of historical memory, and poor accountability for task completion.
scope: Stateless JWT Authentication and RBAC (admin, coordinator, volunteer)
Event CRUD, registration capacity handling, and public event listing
Task assignment with priority, status, and circular dependency checks
Live check-in support with QR codes and attendance dashboards
Operations layer modules: Vendor, Resource, Scheduling, and Budget tracking
goals: Centralize coordination of clubs, events, and operations into a single open platform
Establish a modular, plug-and-play architecture for independent feature development
Provide a secure, reliable, and user-friendly experience for students and organizers
impact: Saves club organizers 10+ hours/week in logistics and coordination overhead
Prevents resource double-booking and detects scheduling conflicts
Maintains institutional memory of past events, budgets, and tasks
roadmap: Phase 6: Growth Layer (Sponsorship deals, marketing campaigns, and dashboards)
Phase 7: System Maturity (In-app notifications, audit logs, file uploads, and analytics)

name: Resource Adda
tagline: Academic resource-sharing platform sorted by branch and semester
description: A full-stack verified repository helping students discover study materials, contribute notes, and connect with academic communities.

repositories.name: Resource-Adda
repositories.description: Full-stack repository containing React frontend and Express backend.

problemStatement: Academic resource discovery is fragmented and unverified. Large file sharing (like textbooks/PDF notes) often fails on standard HTTP routes due to timeouts, and there is no centralized database or department-level moderation workflow.
scope: Hierarchical resource discovery (branch -> semester -> subject -> unit)
Cross-branch search queries and built-in PDF viewer
Contributor uploads with admin-gated moderation queue
Chunked Socket.io file streaming (up to 200MB) directly to Cloudinary
JWT-secured admin panels and daily request analytics logging
goals: Centralize academic materials into a single verified repository
Allow student contributions via a robust moderation flow
Support large note uploads up to 200MB with progress feedback
impact: Speeds up study resource discovery for students
Verifies note quality to block spam and irrelevant materials
Secures admin endpoints and simplifies deployment using Docker Compose
roadmap: Implement automated unit and integration testing suite
Add rate limiting and input sanitization hardening
Integrate email notifications on contribution status changes

name: ArchHira
tagline: Venue booking and approval platform for the Architecture Department
description: A specialized operations and booking engine designed to manage authentication, booking calendars, conflict checks, and admin approvals for Architecture Department venues.

repositories.name: ArchHira
repositories.description: Next.js App Router repository for venue bookings.

problemStatement: Venue booking in the Architecture department is a manual, paper-based, or fragmented process. This results in scheduling conflicts (double-bookings), lack of real-time availability visibility, tedious verification steps, and high administrative load for approvals.
scope: OTP-based authentication for registration, login, and resets
Multi-venue booking calendars (Hira Hall, Arch Hall, Guest House)
Conflict-aware slot validations (First Half, Second Half, Full Day, Room Capacity)
Admin status lifecycle changes with automatic Nodemailer email confirmation alerts
goals: Automate double-booking and availability conflict checks
Provide a visual calendar dashboard showing real-time venue availability
Lower administrative overhead for Department approval workflows
impact: Increases transparency of slot and room availability
Speeds up the booking approval cycle for students and faculty
roadmap: Implement CI testing, component testing, and session security hardening
Add rate limiting for OTP paths and an admin action audit trail
Improve operational observability with structured logging
